title: Schezwan Noodles

subs.name: 200 g rice noodles for a gluten-free version
150 g baby corn or sliced zucchini
100 g cabbage shredded or bok choy

ingredients.name: spring onion, whites and greens separated

subs.name: 3 tbsp sriracha — the heat profile is slightly different but equally bold

steps.text: Cook the noodles in a large pot of salted boiling water for 3–4 minutes until just al dente — they will cook further in the wok. Drain immediately, rinse under cold water to stop cooking, and toss with 1 tsp oil to prevent sticking.
Blanch the broccoli florets in the same boiling water for 90 seconds, drain and plunge into cold water to keep the colour vivid and the texture crisp.
Heat 3 tbsp oil in a wok on the highest flame until just smoking. Add garlic, ginger and green chilli and stir-fry for 20 seconds. Add sliced onion and spring onion whites and cook for 1 minute.

Add mushrooms and stir-fry on high heat for 2 minutes until they release their moisture and begin to brown. Add capsicum and blanched broccoli and toss for 1 minute — keep the broccoli bright green and the capsicum firm.
Add schezwan sauce, soy sauce, vinegar and black pepper. Toss to coat all the vegetables and let the sauce reduce for 30 seconds on the high flame.

Add the cooked noodles and toss vigorously for 2 minutes, working from the bottom of the wok so the noodles do not clump. They should be evenly coated in the deep-red schezwan sauce. Adjust salt, drizzle sesame oil if using, and scatter spring onion greens. Serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: noodles → 200 g rice noodles for a gluten-free version; mushroom → 150 g baby corn or sliced zucchini; broccoli → 100 g cabbage shredded or bok choy; green-chilli-sauce → 3 tbsp sriracha — the heat profile is slightly different but equally bold
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A large kadai on maximum gas flame is a perfect substitute for a wok. Cook in small batches if needed — overcrowding drops the temperature and the noodles will steam instead of fry.
